raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 658 would allow partially disabled veterans to claim the state’s property tax exemption proportional to the disability rating percentage issued by the department responsible for their discharge from service.",
    "Currently, disabled veterans may request a reduction on their home’s assessed value when determining property taxes but only if they are 100% disabled, totally blind, or have lost one or more limbs.",
    "Only a small percentage of veterans are listed as fully disabled.",
    "Even if a veteran were to have a 90% disability rating, they would be ineligible for a property tax exemption.",
    "Many veterans who are severely disabled as a result of their service to the United States are turned away when seeking this exemption.",
    "SB 658 will provide greater fairness for California’s disabled veterans.",
    "Senator Grove hopes the bill will make it easier for veterans to afford the state’s high cost of housing.",
    "As of 2019, the US Department of Veteran Affairs found that an estimated 11,000 veterans were homeless in California."
  ]
}
```
